name: Renormalization Group
core: A transformation that leaves qualitative behavior invariant while changing scale of description

name: Self-Organized Criticality
core: Systems spontaneously evolve toward a critical state where events of all scales occur (power-law distribution)

name: Negative Feedback Stabilization
core: Output is fed back to reduce input deviation, producing homeostasis or oscillation around an attractor

name: Phase Transition / Critical Threshold
core: Continuous parameter change triggers discontinuous qualitative shift in system behavior

name: Symmetry Breaking → Information Gain
core: A symmetric system spontaneously selects one of multiple equivalent states, creating structure from uniformity

name: Resonance Amplification
core: Small periodic forcing at a natural frequency produces disproportionately large response

name: Error-Correcting Codes as Universal Motif
core: Redundant encoding enables reliable information transmission through noisy channels

name: Gödel Incompleteness / Self-Reference Limits
core: Sufficiently powerful formal systems contain true statements they cannot prove about themselves

name: Scale-Free Network Topology
core: Networks where degree distribution follows power law — few hubs, many low-degree nodes — emerge from preferential attachment

name: Quorum Sensing / Collective Threshold
core: Individual agents independently measure local density and trigger collective behavior only above a threshold

name: Fourier Decomposition / Spectral Analysis
core: Any signal can be decomposed into fundamental periodic components, revealing hidden structure in frequency domain

name: Replicator Dynamics
core: Entities that reproduce proportional to fitness dominate; selection pressure drives population composition

name: Critical Slowing Down
core: Near a phase transition, system perturbation recovery time diverges — the system becomes fragile before it breaks

name: Dimensional Reduction / Manifold Hypothesis
core: High-dimensional data concentrates near a lower-dimensional manifold; effective description requires far fewer variables

name: Frustration / Competing Constraints
core: When local constraints cannot all be satisfied simultaneously, the system develops complex ground states and slow dynamics

name: Duality Transform
core: Two apparently different descriptions are exactly equivalent — what is hard in one becomes easy in the other

name: Preferential Attachment / Matthew Effect
core: The rich get richer — probability of acquiring more is proportional to what you already have

name: Dissipative Structures
core: Systems far from equilibrium spontaneously create order by exporting entropy to environment

name: Information Bottleneck
core: Optimal compression preserves only the information relevant to a target variable, discarding noise

name: Turing Pattern / Reaction-Diffusion
core: Local excitation + long-range inhibition produces stable spatial patterns from homogeneous initial conditions

name: Fixed Points of Iterative Maps
core: Repeated application of a transformation converges to or cycles around special invariant states
origin: dynamical systems / Banach fixed point theorem

name: Canalization / Developmental Robustness
core: Biological development is buffered against perturbation — many genotypes map to the same phenotype

name: Hysteresis / Path Dependence
core: System state depends not just on current input but on history — forward and reverse paths differ

name: Ergodicity Breaking
core: Time averages differ from ensemble averages — a system gets trapped in subsets of its state space

name: Superposition / Linear Combination
core: If A and B are solutions, then aA + bB is also a solution — allows construction of complex from simple

name: Lévy Flights / Heavy-Tailed Search
core: Optimal foraging uses many short local steps interspersed with rare long jumps — outperforms pure random walk

name: Maxwell's Demon / Information-Energy Tradeoff
core: Acquiring information about a system requires energy expenditure; using that information can extract work — connecting entropy, information, and thermodynamics

name: Small World Networks
core: High clustering + short path lengths — local cliques connected by long-range shortcuts enable rapid global coordination

name: Catastrophe Theory / Fold Bifurcation
core: Smooth, gradual changes in parameters lead to sudden, discontinuous changes in system behavior at critical manifolds

name: Minimum Description Length / Occam's Razor
core: The best model is the shortest program that reproduces the data — compression = understanding

name: Reservoir Computing / Echo State
core: A high-dimensional dynamical system with fixed random internal connections can compute over temporal inputs with only a trained readout layer

name: Edge of Chaos / Criticality Optimum
core: Computation and adaptation are maximized at the boundary between ordered (frozen) and chaotic (random) dynamics

name: Modularity / Hierarchical Decomposition
core: Complex systems evolve modular structure where subsystems interact strongly within modules and weakly between them

name: Stigmergy / Environment-Mediated Communication
core: Agents communicate indirectly by modifying the shared environment, enabling coordination without direct interaction

name: Topological Protection
core: Certain properties are protected by global topology and cannot be destroyed by continuous local perturbations

name: Percolation Threshold
core: In a random network/lattice, there exists a sharp threshold above which a giant connected component first appears

name: Extremal Optimization / Bak-Sneppen
core: Repeatedly replacing the worst-performing component drives the system to a critical state with power-law distributed avalanches of improvement

name: Adiabatic Invariance
core: When parameters change slowly enough, certain action variables are conserved — allowing controlled state transfers

name: Constructive Interference / Path Integral
core: A quantity is determined by summing contributions from ALL possible paths, with most cancelling and only coherent ones surviving

name: Attractor Landscape / Energy Landscape
core: System dynamics can be understood as descent on a landscape with basins of attraction surrounding stable states

name: Stochastic Resonance
core: Adding noise to a nonlinear system can IMPROVE signal detection — optimal noise level exists

name: No Free Lunch Theorem
core: No algorithm can outperform all others on all possible problems — performance guarantees require assumptions about problem structure

name: Red Queen Effect / Coevolutionary Arms Race
core: Coupled evolving entities must continuously adapt just to maintain relative fitness — standing still means falling behind

name: Aging / Memory in Disordered Systems
core: Systems with many metastable states develop history-dependent responses — older systems respond differently than younger ones to the same stimulus

name: Sensitive Dependence on Initial Conditions
core: Exponential divergence of nearby trajectories makes long-term prediction impossible despite deterministic dynamics

name: Universality Class
core: Microscopically different systems exhibit identical critical behavior if they share the same symmetry and dimensionality

name: Variational Principle / Least Action
core: Nature follows paths that extremize an action functional — unifies diverse phenomena under a single optimization principle

name: Holographic Principle / Boundary-Bulk Correspondence
core: All information contained in a volume can be described by data on its boundary — dimensionality reduction of a fundamental kind

name: Allometric Scaling / Kleiber's Law
core: Biological rates scale as power laws of body mass with universal exponents (often multiples of 1/4)
